ope: interjection. An exclamation of surprise; oops.
ore: noun. Rock or other material that contains valuable or utilitarian materials; primarily a rock containing metals or gems for which it is
ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
oyer: noun. A hearing in a civil case which is based on the content of a document, in which the plaintiff is required to produce the document.
pep: verb. To inject with energy and enthusiasm.
pepo: noun. A fruit of plants of the gourd family Cucurbitaceae, possessing a hard rind and producing many seeds in a single, central, pulpy
perry: noun. A fermented alcoholic beverage made from pears, similar to hard apple cider.
pet: noun. An animal kept as a companion or otherwise for pleasure, rather than for some practical benefit or use.
poet: noun. A person who writes poems.
poetry: noun. Literature composed in verse or language exhibiting conscious attention to patterns and rhythm.
pop: noun. A loud, sharp sound, as of a cork coming out of a bottle, especially when the contents are pressurized by fizziness.
pope: noun. An honorary title of the Roman Catholic bishop of Rome as father and head of his church, a sovereign of the Vatican city state.
popery: noun. The teachings, practices and accoutrements of the Roman Catholic Church.
pore: noun. A tiny opening in the skin.
port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
porter: noun. A person who carries luggage and related objects.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
prey: noun. That which is or may be seized by animals to be devoured.
pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).
prop: noun. An object placed against or under another, to support it; anything that supports.
pry: verb. To peer closely and curiously, especially at something closed or not public.
pryer: noun. A person who pries; a pry.
pyre: noun. A funeral pile; a combustible heap on which corpses are burned.
pyrope: noun. A variety of garnet, of a poppy or blood-red color, frequently with a tinge of orange.
repo: noun. Clipping of repossession, most commonly of a vehicle, house or condominium.
report: verb. To relate details of (an event or incident); to recount, describe (something).
repot: verb. To move (a growing plant) from one pot to a larger one to allow for further growth.

retro: adjective. Of, or relating to, the past, past times, or the way things were.
retry: verb. To try or attempt again.
rope: noun. Thick strings, yarn, monofilaments, metal wires, or strands of other cordage that are twisted together to form a stronger line.
roper: noun. Agent noun of rope; one who uses a rope, especially one who throws a lariat or lasso.
ropery: noun. Any form of language used, typically slang or slander, that can get one in trouble.
ropy: adjective. Resembling rope in appearance or texture, used especially of muscles that are thick or hard to the touch.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rote: noun. Mechanical routine; a fixed, habitual, repetitive, or mechanical course of procedure.
rye: noun. A grain used extensively in Europe for making bread, beer, and (now generally) for animal fodder.
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.

toe: noun. Each of the five digits on the end of the human foot.
top: noun. The highest or uppermost part of something.
tope: verb. To drink excessively; to get drunk.
toper: noun. Someone who drinks alcoholic beverages a lot; a drunkard.
topper: noun. Something that is on top.
tor: noun. A craggy outcrop of rock on the summit of a hill, created by the erosion and weathering of rock.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
toy: noun. Something to play with, especially as intended for use by a child.
toyer: noun. One who toys; one who is full of trifling tricks; a trifler.
trey: noun. A playing card or die with the rank of three.
trop: noun. Alternative form of trope (“cantillation pattern”).
trope: noun. Something recurring across a genre or type of art or literature; a motif.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
tyer: noun. Dated form of tier (“a person or device that ties”).
type: noun. A grouping based on shared characteristics; a class.
tyre: noun. The ring-shaped protective covering around a wheel which is usually made of rubber or plastic composite and is either pneumatic or
yet: adverb. Thus far; up to the present; up to some unspecified time.
epopt: noun. An initiate in the Eleusinian Mysteries; one who has attended the epopteia.
ert: verb. To incite; urge on; encourage.
eyot: noun. A little island, especially in a river or lake.
opter: noun. One who opts, or makes a choice.
ory: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Odia.
petro: noun. A Venezuelan cryptocurrency launched in 2018, supposed to be backed by the country's oil and mineral reserves.
por: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Portuguese.
porer: noun. One who pores, or studies closely.
porret: noun. A scallion; a leek or small onion.
porty: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of port wine.
prore: noun. The front part of a ship.
prp: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Parsi.
pyet: noun. A magpie; a water ouzel.
pyot: noun. A magpie; a water ouzel.
rort: noun. A scam or fraud, especially involving the misappropriation of public money or resources.
rorty: adjective. Boisterous, rowdy, saucy, dissipated, or risqué.
toey: adjective. Agitated, anticipatory, excited (sometimes specifically sexually so), nervous.
trp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kokborok.
yopper: noun. A person employed under the Youth Opportunities Programme in Britain in the 1980s.
yor: adjective. Eye dialect spelling of your.
yot: noun. The letter Ϳ ϳ, an uncommon variant of J j used in Greek linguistics.
yote: verb. To pour water on; pour in.
